title: CensusDataRow Structure
description: Documentation of the JSON shape returned by every Census Data API query. The top-level response is a 2-D array where the first inner array is the header row of variable names and each subsequent inner array is one record.

elements.name: VariableName
elements.description: Variable identifier from the dataset's variables.json dictionary (for example NAME, B01001_001E, state).

elements.name: Cell
elements.description: Cell value at the column index corresponding to the matching Header element. All values are encoded as strings — even numeric measurements — consumers must coerce using predicateType from variables.json.

annotations.geographyColumnConvention: Geography identifier columns (state, county, tract, etc.) are appended after the variables in the `get` clause; total columns = (variables) + (geo hierarchy depth).
